title: Standalone Registry Report
description: Stable JSON payload emitted by `node scripts/report-standalone-registry.mjs --json`. CI tools (annotators, dashboards, slack bots, status pages) MUST validate every payload against this schema before consuming it. Pin against `schemaVersion` to detect breaking changes — a major bump on `schemaVersion` will land alongside an updated `$id` here.

schemaVersion.description: Semver-ish version of the payload shape. Consumers should pin to a major (e.g. accept '1.x'). Bumped on any breaking change to required fields, types, or enums. 1.0 → 1.1: added top-level `locationsRegistry` (additive, minor).

mode.description: Whether the script was invoked with --strict (CI gate) or in report-only mode.

generatedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the report was emitted (Date.prototype.toISOString output).

repoRoot.description: Absolute path to the repository root the report was generated from. Useful when correlating across multiple checkouts on the same runner.

totals.description: Aggregate counts across all scripts. Always present, even when `scripts` is empty (in which case every count is 0).

scripts.description: Total number of standalone scripts discovered under standalone-scripts/ (excluding _generated, types, prompts).

fullyWired.description: Number of scripts with zero gaps. Invariant: `fullyWired + withGaps === scripts`.

withGaps.description: Number of scripts that have at least one missing wiring location.

gapCount.description: Sum of `gaps.length` across every script. Equals 0 iff every script is `fullyWired`.

locations.description: Ordered list of every wiring location the registry checks for. Order matches the column order of the human-readable matrix and is stable across runs of the same `schemaVersion`. Adding a new location requires a `schemaVersion` minor bump; renaming or removing one requires a major bump.

locationsRegistry.description: Self-describing dictionary keyed by every `locations[].key`. Lets external consumers (dashboards, slack bots, CI annotators) render a matrix or explain a gap WITHOUT having to read this script's source code. Every key listed in `locations[]` MUST appear here, and every key here MUST also appear in `locations[]` — `locations[]` defines order, `locationsRegistry` defines meaning. Added in schemaVersion 1.1.

scripts.description: Per-script wiring breakdown, sorted alphabetically by `name`.

exitCode.description: What the process WILL exit with. 1 only when `mode === 'strict'` AND `totals.gapCount > 0`. Embedded in the payload so downstream parsers do not need to capture the process exit code separately (e.g. when reading from an uploaded artifact).

locationKey.description: Stable identifier for a wiring location. New keys may be added in a minor `schemaVersion` bump; existing keys never change meaning. The closed set keeps consumer switch-statements exhaustive.

label.description: Human-readable label for the location, suitable for matrix headers and log output.

locationRegistryEntry.description: Rich descriptor for a single wiring location, used as the value type of `locationsRegistry`. Added in schemaVersion 1.1.

label.description: Mirror of `locationDescriptor.label` — kept here so consumers can render the registry without joining against `locations[]`.

meaning.description: One-sentence explanation of WHAT this location checks and WHY it must exist. Suitable for tooltips and dashboard cell hovers.

fixFile.description: Repo-relative path of the file an engineer must edit to satisfy this location, when the path is fixed across all scripts (e.g. 'package.json'). `null` when the path is per-script (e.g. 'tsconfig.<name>.json' / 'vite.config.<name>.ts') — in that case the per-script path is reconstructed from the script name and is also surfaced via `scripts[].gaps[].fix.file`.

name.description: Folder name under standalone-scripts/ (kebab-case Identifier, e.g. 'payment-banner-hider').

fullyWired.description: True iff every location in `wiring` is `true`. Equivalent to `gaps.length === 0`.

wiring.description: One boolean per location key. Every key listed in top-level `locations[].key` MUST appear here. Additional unknown keys are forbidden so consumers can rely on the closed set.

gaps.description: One entry per location where `wiring[key] === false`. Empty array when `fullyWired` is true. Order matches `locations[]` so consumers can render gaps in the same column order as the human-readable matrix.

label.description: Mirror of the matching `locations[].label` for convenience — saves consumers one lookup.

reason.description: Single-line human-readable explanation of why this location is considered unwired. Suitable for `::error::` annotations and dashboard rows.

fixHint.description: Structured pointer to where and how to fix the gap. `at` is null when the gap is at file-level (e.g. a missing tsconfig file).

file.description: Repository-relative path of the file the consumer should edit (or create).

at.description: JSON path, YAML key, or array name inside `file` where the snippet should be inserted. `null` means the entire file is the fix target (typically a missing config file that needs to be created).

snippet.description: Copy-pasteable code/JSON/YAML fragment that closes the gap. May span multiple lines (newlines preserved).
